title: Algoritmi di Meta e democrazia, S&D: "Ridotta la visibilità dei rappresentanti politici"
snippet: This report details a formal parliamentary question submitted to the European Commission by several S&D MEPs. The inquiry heavily references Fabio Giglietto’s January 2026 study, citing his metrics on how political content suppression reduced Italian parliamentarians' visibility on Facebook by an average of 72%.
description: An article reporting on a formal parliamentary question by S&D MEPs to the European Commission, which heavily cites Fabio Giglietto's January 2026 study on the impact of Meta's algorithms on the visibility of Italian politicians.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: Prof. Fabio Giglietto is featured as a roundtable speaker at the 2026 Social Media & Society Conference in Glasgow, Scotland. He was invited to discuss the practical challenges of using the Meta Content Library (MCL) API for public-interest disinformation and algorithmic transparency research.
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This news article reports on Prof. Giglietto's study, 'A Pretty Blunt Approach: Meta’s Political Content Reduction Policy and Italian Parliamentarians’ Facebook Visibility.' It covers how his computational research on 2.5 million Facebook posts showed a drastic drop in political visibility for mainstream Italian politicians, leading to a formal parliamentary inquiry in Europe and a dedicated investigation by Italy's communications authority (Agcom).
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Utenti profilati e algoritmi: così Meta ha interferito nelle elezioni del 2022
snippet: This investigative report examines how algorithmic profiling and visibility filtering on Meta's platforms affected the 2022 Italian elections, drawing directly upon the findings and data analysis published in Fabio Giglietto's research.
description: An investigative report by Il Fatto Quotidiano exploring algorithmic profiling during the 2022 Italian elections, drawing directly from the research and data analysis of Fabio Giglietto.

title: Report (Puntata del 12/04/2026)
snippet: The investigative journalism TV program 'Report' on Rai3 featured Giglietto's computational social science findings as a focal point. The broadcast highlighted his research on how Meta's recommendation algorithms shifted visibility in Italian and American electoral processes, potentially boosting anti-European political factions.
description: An investigative segment on Rai 3's program 'Report' highlighting Fabio Giglietto's research into Meta's recommendation algorithms and their impact on democracy.

title: Report accusa Meta di interferenze nelle elezioni del 2022: «Così ha profilato gli utenti». La replica dell'azienda
snippet: Media coverage of the Rai3 'Report' investigative episode broadcasted in April 2026. The article highlights how political profiling and algorithmic manipulation (analyzed in studies by experts including Giglietto) impacted the Italian political landscape and voter visibility on Meta's social media platforms.
description: Media coverage of a Rai3 'Report' investigation into Meta's political profiling during the 2022 Italian elections, referencing academic analysis on algorithmic manipulation and coordinated behavior by Fabio Giglietto.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a study by Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, showing how Meta's political content reduction algorithm drastically limited the reach of official political figures (-72% reach for Italian MPs) while inadvertently giving more prominence to extremist and anti-European accounts.
description: An Italian news article reporting on a study by Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino about how Facebook's algorithm reduces the reach of official political figures while boosting extremist content.
